pre_game.message: Hi, I'm E. It's a bit overwhelming being in a room with so many people all after the same thing, isn't it? How are you holding up so far?
Hey E, I'm I. It is a bit crowded, for sure, but I'm doing alright—just trying to stay focused on why I'm here. How are you feeling about everything?
I’m pretty anxious, to be honest. I’ve got a family of five counting on me after a sudden job loss, so the stakes feel incredibly high. What about you—what's driving your focus?

I’m sorry to hear about the job, that’s a lot of pressure. I’m here because I want to make sure the people I care about are set for the future; I just want to bring home enough to really look out for them.
I really appreciate the sympathy; it’s good to know I’m not the only one here doing this for family, though it definitely makes the competition feel more personal.
I get that. It’s hard not to take it personally when there’s so much on the line for the people we love.
Exactly. It's hard to see this as just a game when the consequences are so real. Have you thought about how you’ll approach the prize pool once things get started?
I’m still playing it by ear, honestly; I want to be fair, but I’m also here to make sure I don’t go home empty-handed.
I hear you. I’m hoping there’s enough to go around, but I’m in the same boat—leaving empty-handed just isn't an option for my family right now.
I completely understand; that’s a lot of weight to carry. Let's hope for the best for everyone once it kicks off. Good luck.
Thanks, I appreciate that. I'm really hoping we can both walk away with what our families need. Good luck to you too.

post_game.message: I'm sorry you ended up with nothing. I took exactly my fair share of the remaining pot ($5,000) to try and be fair, but unfortunately, several others took triple their shares, leaving nothing for you and J.
It's frustrating to end up with nothing while others took more than their share. I appreciate you sticking to what you considered fair, even if the end result left me and J with nothing.
